questions.text: I enjoy solving puzzles, riddles, or brain teasers.
I like working with numbers, data, or statistics.
I often question information and try to verify if it’s true.
I enjoy subjects like Maths, Science, or Logical Reasoning.
I like breaking big problems into smaller parts to solve them.
I am comfortable interpreting graphs, charts, or tables.
I enjoy comparing different options and choosing the most efficient one.
I stay calm and think logically even under pressure.
I like understanding why and how things work, not just the final answer.
I can quickly spot mistakes or inconsistencies in calculations or arguments.
I like creating something new – stories, designs, music, or art.
I often think of multiple ways to do the same task.
I pay attention to aesthetics – colours, layouts, or visual appeal.
I enjoy activities like drawing, composing, designing, or editing.
I get excited by brainstorming and idea generation sessions.
I prefer open-ended tasks rather than step-by-step fixed instructions.
I can imagine future scenarios or concepts that don’t exist yet.
I get bored doing very repetitive, routine work.
People often come to me for original ideas or a “fresh perspective”.
I enjoy experimenting with new styles, formats, or creative tools.
I feel confident speaking in front of groups.
I find it easy to start conversations with new people.
I enjoy motivating, guiding, or leading others in group work.
I often take initiative in group discussions or projects.
I am comfortable handling disagreements or differing opinions.
I am interested in understanding what people want or need.
I enjoy roles where I have to convince, negotiate, or sell ideas.
I like networking and building useful relationships with people.
I prefer working with people rather than working completely alone.
Others see me as confident, outgoing, or influential.
I like understanding how machines, gadgets, or tools work.
I enjoy building, assembling, fixing, or tinkering with things.
I prefer clear, practical tasks over very theoretical discussions.
I am comfortable following technical diagrams, manuals, or instructions.
I like using technology to solve real-world problems.
I am interested in fields like engineering, IT, infrastructure, or logistics.
I prefer seeing visible, tangible results of my work.
I can learn to use new tools, software, or equipment relatively quickly.
I pay attention to accuracy and safety while working with equipment.
I would enjoy roles that involve operations, systems, or process improvement.
I try to understand how others are feeling before reacting.
I feel satisfied when I help someone solve a personal or academic problem.
I am patient when explaining things to others.
I am interested in the well-being and growth of people around me.
I would like a career where I can support, protect, or guide others.
People often come to me for advice or emotional support.
I am willing to put in effort to help someone, even if it doesn’t benefit me directly.
I am comfortable listening to others’ problems without judging them.
I enjoy mentoring, teaching, or coaching people.
I feel a sense of responsibility towards society and my community.
